parsed_review.summary: CP00-411 is a clean, internally-consistent descriptor-only closeout pack at the read-only review stage; the pack-level claim is supported and all required boundaries hold. Identity and structure agree across manifest, review-baseline, the registry binding (packages/payments/src/registry.js:324), the contract projection (contracts/payments-core-contract.json:21001), the closeout plan, and the validator: pack_id CP00-411, risk_class A, 10 units, range RP13.P07.M05.S13-RP13.P07.M05.S22, deliverable distribution {failure_recovery:1, implementation:2, hermes_evidence:2, fixture:1, test:2, security_audit:1, claude_review:1}=10 and micro_title {Permission And Audit Binding:10}. Live cursor (closeout-pack-plan.json live_state) is CP00-410 with next_subphase RP13.P07.M05.S13, which is exactly CP411's first unit; handoff to CP00-412 / RP13.P07.M06.S01 is consistent. Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in freezeCp411Result (service.js:2418), validators (validators.js:3507), and the no-write attestation: dispatches_*_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, writes_product_state, creates/updates_database_rows, reads/writes_object_storage, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, executes_command_runtime, loads_real_fixture_data all false; synthetic_only/no_real_data true. Claude stays a read-only reviewer and is not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, read_only=true, tools Read/Grep/Glob), matching the supplied authority boundary, and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt validation, not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). production_ready is correctly false for the descriptor layer (runtime_ready=false), so the production_ready gate is not weakened. Tests (test/model.test.js:1312-1360) and the RP13 validator cover coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, and handoff, and command-evidence records all implementation/plan/repo gates at exit 0 with post-review-gated commands (closeout-pack:validate) correctly deferred. CP178+ hardening is present (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/trust claims); no forged receipts exist (valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts empty) and post-review artifacts (raw-output.json, review-receipt.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this stage. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 informational note about expected-but-deferred post-review artifacts.

findings.title: Manifest references post-review evidence artifacts that are not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: The manifest evidence_refs/required_pack_artifacts and review_hardening point to docs/closeout-packs/cp00-411/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-411/raw-output.json and review-receipt.json. Glob confirms only manifest.json and command-evidence.json exist in the pack dir, and raw-output.json/review-receipt.json are absent. This is the correct pre-review state: these files are produced by stages that run after the read_only_claude_run (normalize, receipt_validate, finding_adjudication, construction_inspection per pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence). It is not a defect in the implementation under review, but the references are unresolved until the closeout sequence completes.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the hardened review sequence produces and validates these artifacts (receipt normalization/validation, adjudication, construction inspection) before npm run closeout-pack:validate and the final commit; do not commit CP00-411 while any required post-review artifact is missing or while valid_review_receipts remains empty.
findings.post_review_disposition: expected_non_blocking: verified these artifacts are generated by sequence stages after this read-only review; absence now is correct and does not block the pack-level production_ready (false) descriptor claim.
